stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The listing provides technical metadata such as the model name and associated file formats (safetensors).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML topics including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
The focus is on a specific, powerful instruction-tuned language model from Meta.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: The paper identifies 'system scaling' as the next major bottleneck in agentic AI, moving beyond just model scaling.
It proposes 'scaling the harness,' which treats the structured execution layer around foundation models as a primary design focus.
This shift emphasizes designing auditable, persistent, modular, and verifiable architectures for AI agents.

stories.title: From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI
stories.author: Shangding Gu
bullet_points: The paper identifies 'system scaling' as the next major bottleneck in agentic AI, moving beyond just model scaling.
It proposes 'scaling the harness,' treating the structured execution layer around foundation models as a primary design focus.
This shift emphasizes creating auditable, persistent, modular, and verifiable architectures for AI agents.

stories.title: DiscoverPhysics: Benchmarking LLMs for Out-of-the-Box Scientific Thinking
stories.author: Carolina Cuesta-Lázaro
bullet_points: Introduces DiscoverPhysics, a new benchmark designed to test genuine scientific reasoning in LLMs.
The benchmark challenges LLMs to discover physical laws in simulated worlds with non-standard physics (e.g., fractional-power gravity).
Addresses the critical issue of distinguishing between LLM recall of existing science versus true out-of-the-box scientific discovery.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks, indicating its LLM nature.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data processing occurs entirely on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining user privacy and local control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are available under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: Cox Media fined after bragging it spied on users through their phones
stories.author: Adi Robertson
bullet_points: Cox Media and associated marketing firms were fined by the FTC.
The controversy stemmed from claims that the companies were secretly listening to users via phones and smart devices.
The fine addresses deceptive practices regarding user privacy and data collection.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing new methods to exploit the 'personalities' of AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from the 'laughably simple' initial hacking of first-generation AI.
The story highlights growing security concerns surrounding the behavioral and persona layers of large language models.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep
stories.author: Giulia Fanti
bullet_points: Addresses the scalability issue of Transformer attention mechanisms with long context lengths.
Proposes a 'sleep-like consolidation mechanism' where recent context is converted into persistent fast weights.
Involves offline recurrent passes over accumulated context to update state-space model (SSM) blocks.

stories.title: StakeBench: Evaluating Language Understanding Grounded in Market Commitment
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Introduces StakeBench, a novel evaluation framework for financial NLP.
Unlike existing benchmarks, StakeBench grounds language understanding in verifiable market commitment.
It links language comments to actual market behavior (positions, actions, and odds) from platforms like Polymarket.

stories.title: Forgetting in Language Models: Capacity, Optimization, and Self-Generated Replay
stories.author: Andrew Gordon Wilson
bullet_points: The paper addresses catastrophic forgetting in language models, where performance on old tasks degrades when learning new ones.
It proposes that self-generated samples from the model's own training distribution can effectively act as replay data to mitigate forgetting.
The study finds that forgetting persists primarily when the model has limited remaining capacity.

stories.title: Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Introduces Auto Benchmark Audit (ABA), an agentic framework designed to verify AI benchmarks.
ABA systematically audits benchmark tasks to uncover flaws like hidden dependencies and specification gaps.
Addresses the growing problem where complex AI benchmarks contain implicit assumptions that traditional methods miss.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to support local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: A battery-powered Starlink Mini is likely on the way
stories.author: Thomas Ricker
bullet_points: SpaceX is reportedly developing a battery-powered version of the Starlink Mini dish.
This new version aims to provide untethered, portable internet access.
Potential users include vanlifers, emergency responders, and travelers needing global connectivity.

stories.title: Ferrari reveals its first EV, with design help from Jony Ive
stories.author: Adi Robertson
bullet_points: Ferrari has unveiled its first electric vehicle, the Luce.
The design of the Luce was developed in collaboration with Jony Ive and Mark Newson of LoveFrom.
This marks Ferrari's entry into the EV market and its second four-door model.

stories.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The US government's investment in quantum computing faces potential legal challenges.
A new quantum foundry company has been launched as part of the initiative.
The article questions the immediate necessity or market need for the newly established quantum foundry.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was reported as 'mostly successful' in its initial test.
The vehicle is still considered a work in progress and has significant hurdles before reaching low-Earth orbit.
The report emphasizes that more testing and proving is required from SpaceX.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Four Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to target an ICEYE radarsat.
The capability described is noted as unusual for satellites performing standard missions.
The story touches upon geopolitical tensions intersecting with advanced space surveillance technology.

stories.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
stories.author: Zhaoxiang Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MobileGym, a browser-hosted simulation platform designed for mobile GUI agent research.
Enables verifiable outcomes via deterministic state-based judging using structured JSON state.
Supports scalable online Reinforcement Learning (RL) through low-cost, parallel rollouts.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in subject-driven image generation: preserving the identity of a subject while adhering to text prompts.
Existing methods often fail due to separate encoding of text and reference images, leading to poor cross-modal reasoning and artifacts.
The proposed work conditions diffusion models to improve identity preservation, an area often overlooked by recent multimodal framework improvements.

stories.title: Beyond Summaries: Structure-Aware Labeling of Code Changes with Large Language Models
stories.author: Karen Yorav
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of manual code review due to the scale of modern software projects.
It proposes a method for structure-aware labeling of code changes (e.g., renames, moves, logic mods) using LLMs.
The goal is to enhance review efficiency through better prioritization and automation of code patches.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs and ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for multiplier-free quantization.
Focuses on ultra-low bit Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace costly MAC operations with efficient bit-shifts.

stories.title: Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World
stories.author: Dandan Tu
bullet_points: Introduces 'Claw-Anything,' a new benchmark for evaluating always-on personal assistant LLM agents.
Addresses the limitation of current systems that only access narrow slices of a user's digital world.
Aims to capture performance in broad, context-sensitive, always-on assistant scenarios.

stories.title: VeriTrace: Evolving Mental Models for Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Alexandra Brintrup
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of managing vast, uncertain information in deep research agents.
Critiques current systems for relying on implicit LLM reasoning for intermediate representations.
Proposes the need for explicit regulation of an agent's 'mental model' to prevent error propagation.

stories.title: Squeezing Capacity from Multimodal Large Language Models for Subject-driven Generation
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of subject-driven image generation, aiming to synthesize new images while preserving a specific subject's identity.
Existing methods often fail at cross-modal reasoning and suffer from artifacts because text and reference images are encoded separately.
The proposed solution conditions diffusion models to better integrate identity preservation with textual instructions, improving upon current multimodal frameworks.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to solve engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is a critical area of research needed for MLLMs to adapt continuously to new, real-world tasks beyond initial training.
Current MCIT methods suffer because they require direct modification of the base MLLM codebase, which Prism aims to circumvent.

stories.title: Looped Diffusion Language Models
stories.author: Dongmin Park
bullet_points: Introduces LoopMDM, a novel architecture for Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) to address underexplored transformer design in this area.
The core innovation involves selectively looping early-middle transformer layers during training.
This looping technique demonstrably improves both the training efficiency and the overall performance of MDMs.

stories.title: OrpQuant: Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for Multiplier-Free Power-of-Two Transformer Quantization
stories.author: Tao Luo
bullet_points: Addresses deployment constraints of LLMs/ViTs on edge devices due to memory and MAC bottlenecks.
Proposes 'OrpQuant,' a method using Geometric Orthogonal Residual Projection for quantization.
Focuses on ultra-low bit, Power-of-Two (PoT) quantization to replace MACs with hardware-efficient bit-shifts.

stories.title: MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research
stories.author: Zhaoxiang Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces MobileGym, a browser-hosted simulation platform for mobile GUI agent research.
Enables verifiable outcomes via deterministic, structured JSON state judging, addressing fidelity issues.
Supports scalable online Reinforcement Learning (RL) through low-cost parallel rollouts.

stories.title: Prism: A Plug-in Reproducible Infrastructure for Scalable Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
stories.author: Da-Wei Zhou
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Prism,' a plug-in infrastructure designed to address engineering bottlenecks in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT).
MCIT is a critical area of research needed for real-world deployment, allowing Large Language Models (MLLMs) to continuously adapt to new tasks.
Current MCIT methods are limited because they require direct, complex modifications to the base MLLM codebase.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep
stories.author: Giulia Fanti
bullet_points: Addresses the scaling limitation of Transformer attention mechanisms with long context lengths.
Proposes a 'sleep-like consolidation mechanism' where recent context is converted into persistent fast weights.
Involves offline recurrent passes over accumulated context to update state-space model (SSM) blocks.

stories.title: StakeBench: Evaluating Language Understanding Grounded in Market Commitment
stories.author: John Cartlidge
bullet_points: Introduces StakeBench, a new evaluation framework for financial NLP.
It shifts focus from external observer labels to language understanding grounded in actual market commitment.
Supervision for StakeBench is derived from observable market behavior, linking comments to verified trading records on platforms like Polymarket.

stories.title: When Gradients Collide: Failure Modes of Multi-Objective Prompt Optimization for LLM Judges
stories.author: Abhishek Divekar
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of multi-objective prompt optimization for LLM judges, where multiple evaluation criteria must be optimized simultaneously.
Existing gradient methods produce natural language critiques, preventing the application of standard multi-task learning conflict-resolution toolkits (like PCGrad).
The research tests various decomposition modes of textual gradient optimizers to resolve these multi-objective conflicts.

stories.title: Peak-Then-Collapse and the Four Interface Channels of Knowledge-Graph Tool Use
stories.author: Dimitar Kazakov
bullet_points: Researchers observed a 'peak-then-collapse' pattern in RL-based tool use when using a knowledge-graph API with Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct.
The policy's tool-grounded answer rate initially improved from 3.8% to 9.6% but subsequently dropped to 0% in a short window.
The study investigates four recurring failure modes across different reward designs when integrating LLMs with structured knowledge graphs.

stories.title: CausaLab: A Scalable Environment for Interactive Causal Discovery Toward AI Scientists
stories.author: Hao Peng
bullet_points: Introduces CausaLab, a new scalable environment designed for evaluating interactive causal discovery.
The environment specifically tests LLM agents on two fronts: problem-solving using causal evidence and hypothesis correctness.
Agents interact with a synthetic laboratory, receiving data, performing interventions, and making predictions.

stories.title: Cursor 3 ships parallel AI agents. Here is the multi-agent workflow that actually works.
stories.author: thegdsks
bullet_points: Cursor 3 introduces parallel AI agents, indicating advancements in autonomous coding assistance.
The article details a specific, functional multi-agent workflow within the new version.
This represents a practical application of multi-agent systems in the developer tooling space.

stories.title: CKP LLM: The Missing Layer Between Your AI Agent and Its Knowledge Base
stories.author: alessandro_marocchini
bullet_points: The article introduces 'CKP LLM,' a proposed solution addressing a gap between AI agents and their knowledge bases.
The problem highlighted is AI agents providing confident but factually incorrect information (hallucination/contextual error).
This suggests a focus on improving grounding and retrieval mechanisms for autonomous AI agents.
